title: ECDH, X25519, and Authenticated Key Agreement
description: A device protocol treats ECDH output as an encryption key, never authenticates peer keys, accepts malformed encodings, and reuses ephemeral keys indefinitely. Learners cumulatively produce authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use supported ECDH curves through Go interfaces with private/public key separation, strict parsing, and shared-secret error handling.
Select X25519 or approved NIST curves from protocol, hardware, compliance, interoperability, and implementation constraints.
Feed the ECDH result and full context into a labeled KDF rather than using raw shared bytes directly.
